rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope before use.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with expected types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing invalid or untyped expressions in guarantee or assign statements.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers are properly declared or visible.
Ensure type consistency in binary expressions and assignments to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations and expressions.
When diagnostics report '<error>' types, trace back to the first unresolved reference and correct it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE requires typed expressions in guarantees and assignments; invalid types often stem from unresolved identifiers.
Use `eq` helpers or `const` declarations to define visible types and values before referencing them in contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve missing references; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
